data.description:
## Context
bridge/_outbound_handlers.py handle_status_event (line ~140) currently calls `acli_client.update_issue(jira_key, status=compiled_status)` for all status changes. When compiled_status is 'deleted', this needs to be intercepted BEFORE the update_issue call (since _LOCAL_STATUS_TO_JIRA has no 'deleted' key — a missed intercept would raise an API error).

acli-integration.py has no delete_issue() method yet. bridge-inbound.py has no guard against writing events for locally-deleted tickets.

## Tests to write
Create `tests/unit/test_bridge_delete_integration.py` (following pattern of existing bridge tests).

### test_status_handler_calls_delete_issue_when_compiled_status_is_deleted (DD outbound 200 path)
- Mock acli_client with delete_issue method that returns success
- Provide a STATUS event for a ticket with compiled_status = 'deleted' and a SYNC file with jira_key
- Call handle_status_event
- Assert acli_client.delete_issue called with the correct jira_key
- Assert acli_client.update_issue NOT called

### test_status_handler_delete_treats_404_as_success (DD 404 idempotent)
- Mock acli_client.delete_issue to raise a 404-equivalent error
- Call handle_status_event with compiled_status = 'deleted'
- Assert no BRIDGE_ALERT written, no exception raised

### test_status_handler_delete_writes_bridge_alert_on_403 (DD 403 BRIDGE_ALERT)
- Mock acli_client.delete_issue to raise a 403-equivalent error
- Call handle_status_event with compiled_status = 'deleted'
- Assert a BRIDGE_ALERT event is written to the ticket directory
- Assert no exception raised (bridge continues processing)

### test_status_handler_delete_skips_when_no_jira_key (DD missing jira_key)
- Setup a ticket with no SYNC file (never synced)
- Call handle_status_event with compiled_status = 'deleted'
- Assert acli_client.delete_issue NOT called
- Assert no BRIDGE_ALERT written (silent skip with debug log)

### test_inbound_guard_skips_deleted_ticket (DD inbound guard)
- Mock a local ticket with status = 'deleted' (tombstone or compiled state)
- Call process_inbound or the relevant inbound handler
- Assert no events written to the ticket directory
- Assert a debug-level log entry is emitted

## Files to create
- `tests/unit/test_bridge_delete_integration.py` — new RED test file

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 tests fail RED before T2/T3 are applied
- Tests use pytest patterns matching existing bridge test files
- Mock acli_client, no real Jira API calls

## Testing mode
RED — defining behavioral contract for bridge delete integration
data.title: Write RED integration tests for Jira bridge delete paths
